title: jCodeMunch retrieval verdict
description: The unified _meta.verdict block attached to retrieval responses (search_symbols, search_text, get_ranked_context). Grounded symbolic retrieval proves absence: an 'absent' state carries the scan counts that back the claim, and 'degraded' means the scan was partial so absence is NOT proven.

state.description: ok = confident matches; low_confidence = non-empty but below threshold; absent = nothing matched and the scan was complete; degraded = partial scan (timeout, or semantic requested with no embedder) — absence not proven.

best_score.description: Best raw match score backing the state, null when no scoring ran.

did_you_mean.description: Near-miss suggestions when the state is absent/low_confidence.

scorer.description: Version pin for the scoring heuristics behind state/best_score. Calibration claims (benchmarks/calibration/planted_queries.json) are pinned to this version; a scorer change without a re-measured artifact fails CI.

coverage.description: Coverage contract backing an absence claim (attached on absent/degraded states only). Discloses what the corpus EXCLUDED at index time so a scan count cannot lie by omission. Absent block = coverage unknown (pre-upgrade index), never 'nothing was excluded'.

generation.description: Index generation the scan ran against.

git_head.description: Truncated HEAD SHA at index time.

included_scopes.description: Git-root-relative subdir prefixes walked into this index; [""] = full walk.

excluded.description: Nonzero skip counts by reason from the last full discovery walk (wrong_extension, too_large, binary, secret, symlink, skip_dir [directories], file_limit).

no_symbols_files.description: Files indexed but yielding zero symbols (parser gaps included).
